Redfin
redfin.com ↗

Redfin is the tech-forward real estate brokerage — combines technology with employed real estate agents. Public since 2017. The interview emphasizes the intersection of technology and real-world real estate operations.

Process

Recruiter screen → 60-minute coding phone (DSA medium) → onsite virtual: 2 coding, 1 system design, 1 craft deep-dive, 1 behavioral. Cycle: 3–4 weeks.

What they actually ask

- Design real-time pricing intelligence for buyers and sellers

- Design agent matching and recommendation

- Design tour scheduling and routing

- Coding: medium DSA, often with geo or scheduling framing

- Behavioral: customer focus, ownership, mission-driven culture

Levels and comp (2026)

- SE II: $140K–$175K total

- Senior SE: $205K–$270K

- Staff: $295K–$385K

- Principal: $410K–$540K

Prep priorities

- Be fluent in Java or Python (the bulk of the codebase)

- Understand real estate operations and broker workflows

- Brush up on geographic indexing and routing algorithms

Frequently Asked Questions

Is Redfin remote-friendly?

Hybrid in Seattle (HQ); some remote within US. Less remote-first than Zillow.

How does Redfin compare to Zillow?

Redfin owns brokerage operations; Zillow is search + leads. Redfin has more operational complexity. Comp is comparable; Zillow pays slightly more on average.

What is the engineering culture?

Mission-driven (transparency in real estate). Strong work-life balance. More employee/agent-focused than typical tech.
